This is the most beautiful scarf in the market.
That one is the cheapest thing on the stall.
The blue tile is the most popular souvenir in Lisbon.
Is this the best price you can offer?
The wool one is more expensive than the cotton one.
The silk one is the most expensive of the three.
The cotton one is the cheapest, and it is the lightest.
Can I try this on, please? Where is the fitting room?
Do you accept credit cards, or is it cash only?
In my opinion, this is the best market in the city.
No, thank you. I am just looking.
It is beautiful, but it is too expensive for me.
Thank you, but not today. Maybe on Saturday.
Good morning, madam! Come, come. Everything on this market stall is handmade, here in Lisbon. Are you looking for something special?
Good morning. Thank you, I am just browsing for now. But I am looking for a souvenir for my son.
Take your time, please. These blue tiles are the most popular souvenir in Lisbon: everybody takes one home. And this scarf here is the most beautiful thing I have.
It is beautiful. But there is no price tag on it. How much is it?
Forty-five euros. It is silk, from a small workshop behind the church, and nothing on this stall is better. The cotton one next to it is the cheapest: twenty euros.
I am sorry, I did not catch that. Could you say the price more slowly, please?
Of course. Four, five. Forty-five for the silk one. Two, zero. Twenty for the cotton one. And if you take two, I can give you a discount.
In my opinion, the silk one is the nicest. I would like both, then. Is this the best price you can offer?
Sixty for the two. That is my best price, madam, and I am not going lower. Cash or card?
Cash, please. And could you wrap the blue one? It is a gift. May I have the receipt, too?
Here you are. Keep the receipt: if your son does not like the color, come back in fourteen days and change it. I am here every Saturday. Enjoy Lisbon!
There is no price tag. How much is it, please?
In my opinion, this is the most beautiful one.
Could you wrap it, please? And may I have the receipt?

Good morning, and welcome to the Feira da Ladra, the oldest market in Lisbon. Please listen to this information before you start. First, the days. This market is open on Tuesday and on Saturday only, from nine in the morning until six in the evening. Saturday is the busiest day, and it is also the most beautiful, because every stall is open. If you do not like crowds, come on Tuesday morning. Second, money. Most of the stalls here take cash only. There is one machine for money at the entrance, next to the church, and the line there is long on Saturday, so bring your cash from the hotel. The big shops in the street behind the market take cards, but the stalls, no. Third, the price. Many things here have no price tag. That is normal in this market: you look, you ask, and the seller tells you the price. And yes, you can bargain. It is not rude here. Ask for a small discount, and ask with a smile. But please, do not bargain on the food, and do not bargain for one euro. Fourth, and this is the most important thing on this recording: you can look at everything, and you can buy nothing. Nobody here is angry with that. Just say, no, thank you, I am just looking, and walk on. Last, your receipt. Ask for it, and keep it. Without the receipt you cannot change anything. Thank you very much, and enjoy the oldest market in the city.

Hello. My name is Cristina, and this is my stall. I am here every Saturday, for nineteen years. Can I tell you something? Many tourists are afraid of me. It is true! They see my stall, they look at my scarves for two seconds, and then they walk very fast, and they look at the ground. And I always think, oh, please, do not be afraid. I am not a lion. I am a woman with scarves. Look, I am going to tell you the secret of this market, and it is very short. You can look at everything, and you can buy nothing. That is not a problem for me. It is my job! People look all day, and ten people buy. That is a normal Saturday. So when you do not want it, please say it. Say, no, thank you, I am just looking. Say it in English, say it with a smile, and I am going to smile back at you, and that is the end. Nobody is angry. Nobody is offended. The only thing I do not like is when a person cannot say no, and buys my most expensive scarf, and goes home unhappy. That is terrible. I do not want your money like that. And one more thing. The most beautiful scarf on my stall is not the most expensive one. It never is. The best thing here is the one you love, and it costs twenty-two euros. So take your time. Look at everything. And ask me the price: that is what I am here for.
